title: Purification of anti-anti-SpA antibody (Ab-2) using SpA-bearing Staphylococcus aureus cells.
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8n4nl","text":"A novel method was designed for the purification of Ab-2 from undiluted WSF of hyperimmune eggs. A total of six preparations were made. ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Mix in an Eppendorf microtube 0.9 ml of undiluted WSF from immunized birds with 25 μl of SpA-bearing Staphylococcus aureus cells (Sigma-Aldrich).
Incubate the microtube at 37 o C for 30 min.
After the incubation period, centrifuge the microtube in an Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge for 5 min.
Observe the microtube that presents a pellet of Ab-1 bound-cells at the bottom ,and the supernatant, where Ab-2 is present.
Decant the Ab-2 containing supernatant to another tube.
. Store it at -20 o C until further use.
creator: Angel A Justiz-Vaillant
